economic_notes: Samahquam receives gaming revenue from BCFN Gaming Revenue Limited Partnership. Mining royalty fees reported. BC Hydro payments received. Enterprise Fund consolidated. Community Trust Fund and Ottawa Trust Funds held by third parties.
notes: Audit report dated September 20, 2024 for fiscal year ended March 31, 2022 — significant delay in audit completion. Due from former employee of $93,919 related to unauthorized expenditures subject to Supreme Court of BC ruling. Assets under construction of $1,108,404 included in tangible capital assets not yet amortized. Six funds consolidated including Enterprise Fund. Ottawa Trust Funds ($442,124) held in trust by Government of Canada under Indian Act. Honoraria of $130,236 paid (likely to Chief and Council) but no formal remuneration schedule provided. CMHC revenue significantly higher in 2022 ($775,348 vs $18,139 in 2021) due to new mortgage proceeds of $740,027 recorded as program revenue. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + CMHC + Province of BC + First Nations Health Authority + Southern Stl'atl'imx Health Society + St'at'imc Trust + BCFN Gaming + BC Hydro + CRI FireSmart = 1,847,964 + 775,348 + 234,680 + 89,059 + 66,637 + 234,506 + 327,735 + 8,774 + 25,302 = 3,609,005; OSR adjusted accordingly.
